metadata.name: Emerge — Elder Deep-Fiend cast by sacrificing Scroll Thief (MV 3)
metadata.description: P1 casts Elder Deep-Fiend ({8}, Emerge {5}{U}{U}) using the Emerge alternative cost (CR 702.119a), sacrificing Scroll Thief ({2}{U}, MV 3) to reduce the Emerge cost by 3. The reduced cost is {5}{U}{U} minus 3 generic = {2}{U}{U}. CR 702.119b: the total cost is reduced by the sacrificed creature's mana value. The sacrifice occurs at cost-payment time (CR 601.2f-h) — Scroll Thief moves to p1's graveyard before Elder Deep-Fiend goes on the stack. Both players pass priority; Elder Deep-Fiend resolves and enters the battlefield as a 5/6 Eldrazi Octopus under p1's control. The cast trigger ('tap up to four target permanents') is stubbed in the card def (TODO: WhenCast trigger not yet implemented) and therefore does not fire. Script confirms: sacrifice at cast time, cost reduction, ETB into correct zone, stack empty after resolution.
metadata.generation_notes:
Elder Deep-Fiend card def (crates/engine/src/cards/defs/elder_deep_fiend.rs): mana_cost {8} (generic: 8), Emerge {5}{U}{U} (AbilityDefinition::Emerge { cost: ManaCost { generic: 5, blue: 2 } }), Flash (KeywordAbility::Flash), cast trigger stubbed as TODO. Power 5, Toughness 6.

Emerge (CR 702.119a): alternative cost — you may cast this spell by sacrificing a creature and paying the emerge cost reduced by that creature's mana value. CR 702.119b: the cost reduction equals the sacrificed creature's mana value (not converted mana cost of a noncreature — it's the same thing for creatures).

Cost calculation: emerge cost = {5}{U}{U}. Scroll Thief MV = 3 ({2}{U}). Reduced cost = {5}{U}{U} - 3 generic = {2}{U}{U}. mana_paid in script = { colorless: 2, blue: 2 }.

Cost payment (CR 601.2f-h): sacrificing Scroll Thief is part of paying the emerge alternative cost. The engine executes this at casting.rs:1957-1965 — move_object_to_zone(sac_id, Graveyard(owner)) before the spell object is placed on the stack. Therefore AFTER the cast action, Scroll Thief is in the graveyard and the stack has 1 object (Elder Deep-Fiend).

Harness action 'cast_spell_emerge': handled in replay_harness.rs:1007-1028. Sets alt_cost: Some(AltCostKind::Emerge), emerge_sacrifice: Some(emerge_sac_id). The 'emerge_sacrifice' JSON field (script_schema.rs:291) is the creature name on the battlefield.

Flash (CR 702.8a): Elder Deep-Fiend has Flash, so it can be cast at any time an instant could be cast. In this script p1 casts during precombat_main phase — Flash is not exercised as a special case here (casting during main phase is also legal for Flash creatures), but it's noted in the card definition.

Cast trigger (tap up to four permanents): stubbed in card def with a TODO comment. The trigger condition WhenCast and multi-target tap effect are not yet implemented. Script does NOT include this tap action and does NOT assert any tapping. See disputes entry.

Why Scroll Thief as sacrifice: MV=3 gives a clean reduction from {5}{U}{U} to {2}{U}{U}, matching the scenario specification. Scroll Thief is a registered CardDefinition (crates/engine/src/cards/defs/scroll_thief.rs) with mana_cost { generic:2, blue:1 }, so enrich_spec_from_def populates it correctly and find_on_battlefield resolves it by name match.

Scenario: turn 4 (flavor — p1 has been ramping), active player p1, precombat_main phase. p1 has {2}{U}{U} in mana pool (colorless:2, blue:2). Scroll Thief on p1 battlefield, Elder Deep-Fiend in p1 hand.

disputes.description: Elder Deep-Fiend's cast trigger ('When you cast this spell, tap up to four target permanents') is not implemented in the card def (TODO comment at elder_deep_fiend.rs:23-26). The trigger requires TriggerCondition::WhenCast (self-cast trigger) and a multi-target tap effect, neither of which exists in the current DSL. Script does not test this trigger. Will need a separate script once WhenCast triggers are implemented.

script.step_note: P1 casts Elder Deep-Fiend using Emerge, sacrificing Scroll Thief (MV 3) to reduce the cost from {5}{U}{U} to {2}{U}{U}.

actions.description: Initial: Elder Deep-Fiend in p1 hand; Scroll Thief on p1 battlefield; stack empty; both players at 40 life
actions.note: P1 has {2}{U}{U} in pool (colorless:2, blue:2). Scroll Thief ({2}{U}, MV=3) is available to sacrifice as the Emerge cost. Elder Deep-Fiend ({8}, Emerge {5}{U}{U}) is in hand.

CR 702.119a: P1 casts Elder Deep-Fiend using the Emerge alternative cost. CR 702.119b: total cost is {5}{U}{U} reduced by Scroll Thief's MV of 3 = {2}{U}{U}. CR 601.2f-h: Scroll Thief is sacrificed as part of cost payment — it moves to p1's graveyard before Elder Deep-Fiend goes on the stack. CR 118.9: alternative cost replaces the normal mana cost {8}.

actions.description: After casting: Elder Deep-Fiend on stack; Scroll Thief in p1 graveyard (sacrificed at cast time); p1 hand empty
actions.note: CR 601.2f-h: Scroll Thief was sacrificed as part of the Emerge cost payment — it is in p1's graveyard before the spell lands on the stack. Elder Deep-Fiend is on the stack. P1's battlefield is empty.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — top of stack (Elder Deep-Fiend) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 608.2: Elder Deep-Fiend resolves and enters the battlefield under p1's control as a 5/6 Eldrazi Octopus. The cast trigger (tap up to four permanents) is stubbed as TODO in the card def and does not fire.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Elder Deep-Fiend resolves as a creature spell — it enters the battlefield. No on-cast trigger fires (WhenCast not yet implemented). Stack becomes empty.

actions.description: Final: Elder Deep-Fiend on p1 battlefield (5/6); Scroll Thief in p1 graveyard; stack empty; both players at 40 life
actions.note: Elder Deep-Fiend (5/6) is on p1's battlefield. Scroll Thief was consumed as the Emerge cost sacrifice. Life totals unchanged — no combat or damage occurred. Confirms: Emerge alternative cost (CR 702.119a), MV-based cost reduction (CR 702.119b), sacrifice-at-cost-payment (CR 601.2f-h), creature resolution onto battlefield (CR 608.2).
